videos.title: Build a community storefront, then complete a sale
videos.summary: Bring sellers into a shared market, help buyers discover the right products, and follow one sale from storefront to completion.
videos.outcome: The completed sale comes back to the community market that made it possible, so its contribution is clear to everyone involved.
videos.disclosure: Recorded on the Mobazha local test network. The operator commission is an attributed estimate, not a withdrawable balance.
videos.appliesTo: Mobazha local E2E test-network build recorded on 2026-07-18

evidence.label: Mobazha public repositories and reviewed demo provenance

chapters.title: Create and publish the market
chapters.description: Maya creates a curated, invite-only market, sets the operator commission, and publishes a buyer-facing page before recruiting sellers.

chapters.title: Invite one seller
chapters.description: The seller opens the invitation, sees the commission terms, and joins the market in one step.

chapters.title: Curate and share
chapters.description: The operator features one coherent product and shares the branded community storefront.

chapters.title: Complete one order
chapters.description: A buyer chooses the featured product and completes the purchase from the community storefront.

chapters.title: Fulfill the same order
chapters.description: The seller receives the paid order, adds tracking, and marks it shipped.

chapters.title: Complete the order
chapters.description: The buyer confirms receipt and the order reaches its completed state.

chapters.title: Trace the operator outcome
chapters.description: The operator opens the attribution view and sees the same order, share source, rate, and estimated commission.

videos.transcript: Maya creates a curated community market, sets a ten percent operator commission, and publishes it. One seller reviews those terms and joins through the invitation link. Maya curates the seller's product and shares the market. A buyer opens that storefront, creates one order, and pays 0.012 ETH to its monitored address on the local test network. The seller accepts and ships the same order. The buyer confirms receipt. Maya then sees that completed order attributed to the community share link with a 0.0020 ETH estimated commission. The estimate is not a withdrawable balance.

videos.title: Share products and earn when they sell
videos.summary: A seller offers a commission, a promoter shares one link, and an attributed order rewards the promoter automatically.
videos.outcome: Kai can see that the sale came through his link and that his commission is ready, without running a separate affiliate workflow.
videos.disclosure: Recorded on the Mobazha local test network. The short-link and QR share surface is labeled Preview while it completes rollout.
videos.appliesTo: Mobazha local E2E test-network build recorded on 2026-07-18

evidence.label: Mobazha public repositories and reviewed demo provenance

chapters.title: Offer promoters a cut
chapters.description: Alice sets a five percent storefront-wide commission and a seven-day attribution window.

chapters.title: Use one link everywhere
chapters.description: Kai sees the five percent return, creates one wallet-bound short link, and uses it for the sale.

chapters.title: Shop through the referral
chapters.description: A buyer follows the link, keeps the referral, and continues through the normal store and checkout experience.

chapters.title: Settle automatically
chapters.description: One completed purchase rewards the seller and promoter without a separate affiliate workflow.

chapters.title: See the reward arrive
chapters.description: Kai sees the Paid commission row, paid-out rollup, and transaction hash.

videos.transcript: Alice offers promoters five percent on every attributed storefront order. Kai sees the concrete return and creates one wallet-bound short link. Jordan follows it, shops normally, and pays the resulting ETH order on the local test network. That payment submits the seller and promoter settlement legs without a second affiliate workflow. Kai's earnings page shows the exact commission as Paid with its basis, buyer, confirmation time, payout address, and on-chain transaction hash. The short-link share surface remains labeled Preview while rollout completes.

videos.title: Turn your store into your brand
videos.summary: Apply a storefront template, choose brand colors and featured products, share a private preview, and publish the same buyer-facing design without code.
videos.outcome: Alice's public storefront shows the same branded palette, featured products, story, and call to action that she reviewed through the private preview.
videos.disclosure: The storefront studio additions are labeled Preview while they complete rollout to the hosted SaaS.
videos.appliesTo: Mobazha storefront-studio Preview build recorded on 2026-07-18

evidence.label: Mobazha public repositories and reviewed demo provenance

chapters.title: See the template problem
chapters.description: Alice starts from a credible product row that still looks like a generic template.

chapters.title: Set the brand foundation
chapters.description: One layout and warm palette establish the hero, featured products, story, and trust sections.

chapters.title: Curate products and story
chapters.description: She selects three coherent cold-storage products, writes the field-testing story, and sets one buyer call to action.

chapters.title: Review a private preview
chapters.description: A logged-out buyer opens the seven-day preview link and sees the exact draft with a Preview banner.

chapters.title: Publish the same design
chapters.description: Alice publishes and verifies the native-density public storefront without the Preview banner.

videos.transcript: Alice starts with a live storefront whose credible cold-storage products still look generic. In the built-in design studio, she applies one complete layout and a warm leather-and-steel palette, selects three coherent products, writes the store story, and sets one buyer call to action. She creates a private preview link and reviews the exact draft as a logged-out visitor. After publishing, a matched product-row shot shows the same branded design without the Preview banner. The storefront studio additions remain labeled Preview while hosted rollout completes.

videos.title: Lock one offer. Get paid. Unlock the file.
videos.summary: Create a protected Deal Link for a digital product, share the locked terms, take one ETH payment, and deliver Access verified from the same order.
videos.outcome: Mira locks one Creator Launch Pack offer; Jordan pays 0.025 ETH once and opens Access verified without a separate delivery email.
videos.disclosure: Recorded on the Mobazha local test network. The delivered launch pack is deterministic test content served by the local E2E delivery endpoint, not a production customer file.
videos.appliesTo: Mobazha local E2E Preview build recorded on 2026-07-20

evidence.label: Mobazha public repositories and reviewed demo provenance

chapters.title: Create a protected offer
chapters.description: Mira picks Creator Launch Pack and locks price, deliverables, and protection into one Deal Link.

chapters.title: Share the locked link
chapters.description: The live link is ready to send — terms freeze at acceptance.

chapters.title: Review the exact promise
chapters.description: Jordan sees Northbound Studio, 0.025 ETH, deliverables, and protection on one page.

chapters.title: Pay once
chapters.description: The order watches the exact ETH payment on the local test network and advances without email chase.

chapters.title: Open what you bought
chapters.description: The launch pack opens with Access verified — real content, not a placeholder.

videos.transcript: Mira at Northbound Studio creates one protected Deal Link for Creator Launch Pack, locking price, deliverables, and buyer protection. She shares the live link. Jordan opens the same offer, reviews Northbound Studio and the 0.025 ETH total, and pays once to a unique monitored address on the local test network. The order advances and grants the real delivery link without a seller review step or a separate email. Jordan opens the deterministic launch pack and sees Access verified.

videos.title: Resolve a protected order fairly
videos.summary: Keep a disputed order protected, review what happened, agree on a fair ruling, and close the order with clarity.
videos.outcome: Everyone can see the decision, the agreed outcome, and that the protected order has reached a clear resolution.
videos.disclosure: Recorded with test accounts and test ETH on the Mobazha local E2E network. The Safe deployment, dispute release, and payout transaction use local Anvil; no production funds are involved.
videos.appliesTo: Mobazha local E2E Preview build recorded on 2026-07-19

evidence.label: Mobazha public repositories and reviewed demo provenance

chapters.title: Open one evidence-backed case
chapters.description: Jordan's claim and uploaded photo stay attached to the exact funded order.

chapters.title: Keep funds protected
chapters.description: The payment remains in the Safe while neither party can silently rewrite the order or take the full balance.

chapters.title: Record a neutral ruling
chapters.description: The moderator reviews the same case, assigns 60 percent to the buyer and 40 percent to the seller, and publishes the reason.

chapters.title: Accept the protected payout
chapters.description: The buyer verifies the three-way amount breakdown before authorizing the dispute release.

chapters.title: Keep the full resolution trail
chapters.description: The completed order retains the evidence, ruling, payout amounts, release hash, and resolved state.

videos.transcript: Jordan opens a dispute on a funded, arbitration-protected ETH order and submits one claim with photo evidence. The payment remains in the Safe during review. The moderator opens the same case record, reviews the product, amount, claim, evidence, buyer, and seller, then records a 60 percent buyer and 40 percent seller ruling with a written reason. Jordan sees the ruling and exact buyer, seller, and moderator amounts before accepting the payout. Mobazha deploys the Safe when needed, submits the dispute-release transaction on local Anvil, and completes the order. The final audit trail retains the evidence, 60/40 ruling, on-chain release hash, and Dispute resolved state.

videos.title: Take your first crypto-paid physical order
videos.summary: Open a ready storefront, accept a verified ETH payment for a physical good, and ship the same order to completion.
videos.outcome: Elena's store completes the same physical order from verified ETH payment through shipping and buyer confirmation.
videos.disclosure: Recorded with test accounts and test ETH on the Mobazha local E2E network (Anvil). Not Outpost, not Monero, and not a public-network payment.
videos.appliesTo: Mobazha SaaS E2E Preview build recorded on 2026-07-19

evidence.label: Mobazha public repositories and reviewed demo provenance

chapters.title: Open a ready storefront
chapters.description: Elena's Forge Signal Supply store and featured physical products are live before the first buyer arrives.

chapters.title: Publish one physical offer
chapters.description: The Titan Leather Hardware Wallet Case shows price, shipping promise, and buyer-visible product detail.

chapters.title: Place the first order
chapters.description: Jordan checks out with item, address, and shipping on the same screen.

chapters.title: Pay the verified total
chapters.description: A unique Safe address watches the exact ETH amount on the local test network.

chapters.title: Ship with tracking
chapters.description: Elena accepts the paid order, adds carrier and tracking, and opens the packing slip.

chapters.title: Confirm receipt
chapters.description: Jordan confirms delivery so the same order reaches its completed state.

chapters.title: First crypto order done
chapters.description: Elena's seller order view holds the completed, paid, and fulfilled outcome.

videos.transcript: Elena opens a dressed SaaS storefront for Forge Signal Supply with a live physical listing. Jordan places one order for the Titan Leather Hardware Wallet Case and pays the exact ETH total to a unique watched address on the local test network. Payment verifies automatically. Elena accepts the paid order, ships with UPS tracking, and reviews the packing slip. Jordan confirms receipt. The same order ends completed on Elena's seller view. Recorded on the Mobazha local test network.
